compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Audit Reliability finding #6 (MEDIUM): REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT / timeout_s is a dead knob — it is defined, documented, and env-resolved (src/rebar/llm/config.py: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S=600 @L280, field @L447, resolved @L519) but never passed to the runner (src/rebar/llm/runner.py), so every LLM call rides the Anthropic SDK's built-in default (~600 s) regardless of the operator's setting. An operator who sets a tighter timeout believes hangs are bounded when they are not; the knob silently lies.

## What
Thread the resolved `cfg.timeout_s` into the runner so it actually bounds each LLM call.

## API surface (grounded — resolves the plan-review T1/T8 mechanism question)
Verified by introspecting the installed **pydantic-ai 1.107.0**: the per-request wall-clock timeout is the `timeout` key on `ModelSettings` / `AnthropicModelSettings` (maps to the underlying httpx/Anthropic client request timeout). There is **no** `inactivity_timeout` kwarg on `Agent.run_sync` in this version (run_sync params: user_prompt, output_type, ..., model_settings, usage_limits, ...). So the correct wiring is to set `model_settings["timeout"]` where runner.py already assembles the `AnthropicModelSettings` (alongside max_tokens / cache flags), which flows to both the string-model path and the explicit AnthropicModel branch.

Default semantics: `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S = 600` equals the Anthropic SDK default (600 s / 10 min), so when the knob is unset the resolved `timeout_s` is already 600 → passing it is never lower than the SDK default. An explicit operator value is honored verbatim (including a higher value); we do not silently raise/lower an explicit value.

## Scope
In: src/rebar/llm/runner.py (set model_settings["timeout"] = cfg.timeout_s), tests (assert the value reaches the passed model_settings), a config-reference/docs note (docs/llm-framework.md) stating the default semantics. Out: retry/backoff on 429/529 (separate audit finding 6.4); workflow-engine step budgets (already enforced); the "fail closed on unreadable config" idea (parked as sluggish-moistful-chital).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cfg.timeout_s` / REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT demonstrably reaches the pydantic-ai model settings (test asserts `model_settings["timeout"] == cfg.timeout_s`, via inspection or a monkeypatched Agent capturing the settings)
- [ ] Unset knob ⇒ effective timeout is the SDK default (600 s) or higher — asserted in the test (no hardcoded lower value); explicit operator value honored verbatim
- [ ] docs/llm-framework.md states the default wiring/semantics
- [ ] `make test` green

file_impact.reason: default semantics: never below SDK default

compiled_state.title: Wire REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT into the LLM runner; default no lower than the SDK default
